ocr.text: In the Microsoft Asia-Pacific report, the R&D expenditure across divisions reveals significant insights, with
AI&MachineLearning showing a total expenditure of HKD1.5B in FY2019, HKD1.8B in FY2020, HKD2.1B in FY2021,
HKD2.5B in FY2022, and HKD3.0B in FY2023, achieving a 5-Year CAGR of 15%. CloudServices follows with expenditures
of HKD2.0B in FY2019, HKD2.4B in FY2020, HKD2.9B in FY2021, HKD3.5B in FY2022, and HKD4.2B in FY2023,
marking a robust 5-Year CAGR of 18%.

The GamingDivision demonstrated expenditures of HKD1.0B in FY2019, HKD1.2B in FY2020, HKD1.4B in FY2021,
HKD1.6B in FY2022, and HKD1.9B in FY2023 with a 5-Year CAGR of 10%. The Total R&D expenditure for the same period
was HKD4.5B in FY2019, HKD5.4B in FY2020, HKD6.4B in FY2021, HKD7.6B in FY2022, and HKD9.1B in FY2023,
achieving an overall 5-Year CAGR of 15%.

Furthermore, the Cloud Infrastructure Spending section highlights that in 2023, revenue reached HKD1.5B, with CAPEX at
HKD600M, OPEX at HKD400M, and net profit at HKD500M, representing a market share of 25%, with total users at 300K
and yearly growth of 12.5%, along with an operating margin of 33%.
